title: [ConstraintElim] Fall back to ICmpInst::compare in checkCondition
author: artagnon
description: The simplification allows further simplifications by ConstraintElim.

tests.test_body:
define i1 @add_nuw_neg_pr54224_i64(i64 %a) {
entry:
  %neg2 = add nuw i64 %a, -305
  %c.1 = icmp ugt i64 0, %neg2
  br i1 %c.1, label %exit.1, label %exit.2

exit.1:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i64 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.2

exit.2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.3 = icmp ugt i64 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.3
}

declare void @use(i1)

define void @loop_iv_cond_variable_bound(i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %t.1 = icmp ule i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %t.1)
  %t.2 = icmp sge i32 %iv, 0
  call void @use(i1 %t.2)
  %t.3 = icmp sge i32 %iv, -1
  call void @use(i1 %t.3)
  %c.1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %c.1)
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.2)
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv, %n
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 1
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i1)

define void @loop_iv_cond_constant_bound() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %t.1 = icmp ule i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %t.1)
  %t.2 = icmp sge i32 %iv, 0
  call void @use(i1 %t.2)
  %t.3 = icmp sge i32 %iv, -1
  call void @use(i1 %t.3)
  %c.1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %c.1)
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.2)
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 1
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i1)

define void @loop_iv_cond_variable_bound(i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %t.1 = icmp ule i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %t.1)
  %t.2 = icmp sge i32 %iv, 0
  call void @use(i1 %t.2)
  %t.3 = icmp sge i32 %iv, -1
  call void @use(i1 %t.3)
  %c.1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %c.1)
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.2)
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.latch, label %exit

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %t.4 = icmp ule i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %t.4)
  %c.3 = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %c.3)
  %c.4 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.4)
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 1
  br label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.header
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i1)

define void @loop_iv_cond_constant_bound() {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %t.1 = icmp ule i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %t.1)
  %t.2 = icmp sge i32 %iv, 0
  call void @use(i1 %t.2)
  %t.3 = icmp sge i32 %iv, -1
  call void @use(i1 %t.3)
  %c.1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %c.1)
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.2)
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.latch, label %exit

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %t.4 = icmp ule i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %t.4)
  %c.3 = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %c.3)
  %c.4 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.4)
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 1
  br label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.header
  ret void
}

define void @multiple_pow2_start_offset_dynamic(i64 %count) {
entry:
  %shl = shl i64 %count, 2
  %end = add i64 %shl, 1
  %precond = icmp ne i64 %end, 0
  br i1 %precond, label %loop, label %exit

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 1, %entry ]
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 4
  %cmp.i.not = icmp eq i64 %iv, %end
  br i1 %cmp.i.not, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop
  %cmp2.i.i = icmp ult i64 %iv, %end
  br i1 %cmp2.i.i, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop, %entry
  ret void
}

define i1 @test_overflow_in_negate_constraint(i8 %x, i64 %y) {
bb:
  %zext = zext i8 %x to i64
  %shl = shl nuw nsw i64 %zext, 63
  %icmp = icmp uge i64 %y, %shl
  ret i1 %icmp
}

define i1 @sub_nuw_i64_simp(i64 %a) {
entry:
  %neg2 = sub nuw i64 %a, 305
  %c.1 = icmp ugt i64 0, %neg2
  br i1 %c.1, label %exit.1, label %exit.2

exit.1:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i64 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.2

exit.2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.3 = icmp ugt i64 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.3
}

define i1 @sub_nuw_i16_simp(i16 %a) {
entry:
  %neg2 = sub nuw i16 %a, 305
  %c.1 = icmp ugt i16 0, %neg2
  br i1 %c.1, label %exit.1, label %exit.2

exit.1:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i16 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.2

exit.2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.3 = icmp ugt i16 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.3
}

define i1 @sub_nuw_neg_i16(i16 %a) {
entry:
  %neg2 = sub nuw i16 %a, -305
  %c.1 = icmp ugt i16 0, %neg2
  br i1 %c.1, label %exit.1, label %exit.2

exit.1:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i16 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.2

exit.2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %c.3 = icmp ugt i16 %a, 0
  ret i1 %c.3
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

define i1 @sgt_known_neg(i8 %idx) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i8 %idx, -1
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp)
  %t.1 = icmp uge i8 %idx, 0
  %t.2 = icmp uge i8 %idx, 1
  %res.1 = xor i1 %t.1, %t.2
  %c.1 = icmp ugt i8 %idx, -1
  %res.2 = xor i1 %res.1, %c.1
  ret i1 %res.2
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }

define i1 @test_pr58009_const_zext() {
entry:
  %ext.t.1 = zext i1 true to i16
  %ext.t.2 = zext i1 true to i16
  %t.1 = icmp uge i16 %ext.t.1, %ext.t.2
  %f.1 = icmp ugt i16 %ext.t.1, %ext.t.2
  %res.1 = xor i1 %t.1, %f.1
  %ext.f.1 = zext i1 false to i16
  %ext.f.2 = zext i1 false to i16
  %t.2 = icmp uge i16 %ext.f.1, %ext.f.2
  %f.2 = icmp ugt i16 %ext.f.1, %ext.f.2
  %res.2 = xor i1 %res.1, %t.2
  %res.3 = xor i1 %res.2, %f.2
  %t.3 = icmp ult i16 %ext.f.1, %ext.t.2
  %f.3 = icmp ugt i16 %ext.f.1, %ext.t.2
  %res.4 = xor i1 %res.3, %t.3
  %res.5 = xor i1 %res.4, %f.3
  ret i1 %res.5
}

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> I don't understand the motivation. Isn't it covered by `InstCombinerImpl::foldICmpUsingKnownBits`?

Just checking if there's a diff in llvm-opt-benchmark -- I think this simplification allows further simplifications within ConstraintElim?

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> > I don't understand the motivation. Isn't it covered by `InstCombinerImpl::foldICmpUsingKnownBits`?
> 
> Just checking if there's a diff in llvm-opt-benchmark -- I think this simplification allows further simplifications within ConstraintElim?

Looks like this is indeed the case?

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Base on llvm-opt-benchmark, this seems to have essentially no impact.

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> Base on llvm-opt-benchmark, this seems to have essentially no impact.

Hm, this is the diff? https://github.com/dtcxzyw/llvm-opt-benchmark/pull/3348/changes

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> > Base on llvm-opt-benchmark, this seems to have essentially no impact.
> 
> Hm, this is the diff? https://github.com/dtcxzyw/llvm-opt-benchmark/pull/3348/changes

Exactly. There are only two files with non-trivial improvements, which is very low for this kind of change. Duplicating a fold that a different pass is responsible for requires stronger motivation.
